name: Deerfield Management Company, L.P.
focus: Deerfield is dedicated to advancing healthcare through investment, intelligence, and philanthropy, deploying flexible capital across the entire healthcare ecosystem from early-stage startups to mature companies. The firm backs promising therapeutics, medical technology, care delivery models, and paradigm-shifting technologies including AI and machine learning to improve health outcomes.

name: Andreessen Horowitz
focus: a16z backs visionary founders building in industries undergoing major technological disruption, guided by the conviction that technology is the most powerful force driving societal progress. The firm focuses on key architectures of the future—AI, crypto, and bio—offering a full-stack operating platform alongside capital across all stages.

name: Lightspeed China Partners
focus: A leading China-focused venture capital firm serving entrepreneurs as a lead investor in early-stage and emerging growth companies, partnering closely with founders to build transformational, sustainable industry leaders in sectors aligned with global innovation trends.

name: TCP Venture Capital
focus: TCP Venture Capital addresses the critical gap in early-stage capital for Baltimore City, investing in high-growth, scalable, capital-efficient technology companies that commit to headquartering in Baltimore in order to generate strong returns while creating high-paying local jobs and stimulating regional economic development.

name: Tola Capital Management, LLC
focus: Tola Capital practices hypothesis-based investing in the next generation of enterprise software companies enabled by artificial intelligence, partnering long-term with founders building transformational products powered by new data and cloud platforms. The firm targets companies with 'real invention'—the right team, technology, large addressable market, and culture to build category-leading, multibillion-dollar businesses.

name: Sequoia Capital Operations, LLC
focus: Sequoia pursues a market-first investing philosophy, targeting large addressable markets and partnering early with outlier founders capable of building generational, category-defining businesses. Through its evergreen fund structure, it backs companies from seed through IPO and beyond as a long-term partner.

name: AIN Ventures (Academy Investor Network)
focus: AIN Ventures is a pre-seed and seed-stage venture fund investing in dual-use deep technology with both commercial and government/national security applications, as well as in veteran-led startups, prioritizing founders with leadership, determination, and grit.

name: General Catalyst Group Management, LLC
focus: General Catalyst partners with ambitious entrepreneurs to drive resilience and applied AI, backing transformative companies from seed to growth stage under CEO Hemant Taneja's 'unscaling' thesis of using data and AI to personalize core societal services. The firm builds foundational enterprise technologies and consumer brands while operating as both a venture fund and transformation sponsor.

name: Insight Venture Management, LLC
focus: Insight Partners is a software-first investor built on the belief that software founders need more than capital to scale: they need operational expertise, talent networks, and strategic guidance delivered through its embedded Insight Onsite team. The firm backs high-growth technology and 'ScaleUp' software companies from seed to IPO, partnering with proven product-market-fit businesses to accelerate growth.

name: Tiger Global Management, LLC
focus: Tiger Global pursues a crossover strategy of identifying high-quality global companies leveraging technological innovation across secular growth trends, deploying patient, long-term capital from early stage through IPO with minimal governance involvement. Its post-2022 strategy emphasizes fewer, larger, high-conviction concentrated bets.

name: QuestMark Partners, L.P.
focus: QuestMark is an expansion-stage growth equity firm that backs exceptional management teams building highly-differentiated, often category-creating companies with strong commercial traction, a preference for recurring revenue models, and the strategic position to support long-term standalone market leadership.

name: Blackbird Laboratories, Inc.
focus: A Baltimore-based nonprofit life sciences incubator and its venture arm (Blackbird BioVentures) that de-risks academic research from institutions like Johns Hopkins and University of Maryland with non-dilutive grants, then invests early equity to spin out and scale biotech companies locally. The goal is to translate breakthrough research into venture-ready therapeutics and build Baltimore into a national biotech hub.

name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
focus: BridgeBio is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company that discovers, develops, and delivers transformative medicines for patients with genetic diseases and cancers with clear genetic drivers, using a hub-and-spoke model that takes many 'shots on goal' across an advantaged R&D space. Note: BridgeBio is primarily a drug-development company rather than a traditional venture capital firm.

name: Early Charm Ventures, LLC
focus: Early Charm is a venture studio that co-founds and operates companies with entrepreneurial researchers, transforming early-stage university scientific discoveries into sustainable, revenue-generating businesses rather than chasing quick exits.

name: Accel (formerly Accel Partners)
focus: Accel employs a 'prepared mind' philosophy, deeply studying specific themes and markets in advance so partners can move quickly to back exceptional founders building category-defining technology companies. It partners with founders from seed through growth stages, providing hands-on support in strategy, hiring, and scaling.
